---
title: "announcing xybuntu!!!"
date: 2006-04-01
forum: The Cafe
---

### Post by cerebrix on 2006-04-01
The ubuntu, kubuntu, and nubuntu projects have covered every base we've needed it to cover it it continues to do so.  We make linux for human beings and its brought us a community that is completely unrivaled.

But what about the not so human?

This is why i've begun developing xybuntu.  Linux for artificial beings.

[IMG]http://emailthatguy.shackspace.com/cybuntu.jpg[/IMG]

Teams are forming now.  If you have the ability to communicate in complete binary please let us know

---

### Post by niviche on 2006-04-01
Will this have anything to do with Frubuntu, the new Apple-developped version of Ubuntu?
I just read an interview with Steve Jobs in which he states that Apple will release a first version of Frubuntu in the fall, and described it as a very hip-looking version of Ubuntu, running exclusively on Apple hardware, and not compatible with anything else?

Quote:
"I am open to innovation and interested by open source. While Frubuntu will use only our specific hardware, we want people to understand that we are open to changes. As a gesture, the Frubuntu logo will feature not only our beloved apple, but also a pear and an orange. That's how open we are".

[img]http://www.outofhere.net/frubuntu.gif[/img]

---

### Post by professor_chaos on 2006-04-01
[QUOTE=briancurtin]there are 2s and 3s in binary now?[/QUOTE]


Yes, theres a new version of binary. You can update with "sudo apt-get update"  "sudo apt-get install binary_version2.0"
